content.value: Cantonese is a tonal language. The pitch of your voice changes the meaning of the word. Let's learn the first three tones: 1, 2, and 3.
Tone 1 (High Level): Like a high, flat note in music. Keep it steady and high.
Tone 2 (High Rising): Start in the middle and slide up to the top, like you're asking a surprised 'What?'.
Tone 3 (Mid Level): Keep your voice flat but at a comfortable middle pitch.

pages.title: Upper Tones Mastered!
pages.summary: You've learned to distinguish high, rising, and mid pitches.
